description: Creates a new note in the simulated TriliumNext note system under a specified parent. Validates the content based on note type, checks for duplicate titles in the same parent folder unless forceCreate is enabled, and assigns a unique note ID and content hash (blobId).
code:
def create_note(
    notes: Annotated[NotesState, Depends("notes")],
    parentNoteId: str,
    title: str,
    type: NoteType,
    content: Optional[str] = None,
    mime: Optional[str] = None,
    attributes: Optional[List[Attribute]] = None,
    forceCreate: bool = False,
) -> str:
    """
    Creates a new note in the simulated TriliumNext note system under a specified parent.

    Validates the content based on note type (e.g., strips HTML for 'text' notes),
    checks for duplicate titles in the same parent folder unless forceCreate is enabled,
    and assigns a unique note ID and content hash (blobId).

    :param parentNoteId: The unique ID of the parent note (folder) where the new note will be created.
    :param title: The title of the new note. Must be non-empty and unique within the parent unless forceCreate=True.
    :param type: The type of the note. Supported values: 'text', 'code', 'render', 'search', 'relationMap', 'book', 'noteMap', 'mermaid', 'webView', 'file', 'image'.
    :param content: Optional plain-text or HTML content of the note. If omitted, defaults to an empty string.
    :param mime: Required only for 'file' or 'image' types. Specifies the MIME type of the file content (e.g., 'image/png').
    :param attributes: Optional list of label or relation attributes to attach to the note.
    :param forceCreate: If True, bypasses the duplicate-title check and allows creation of a note with an existing title in the same parent.

    :return: A human-readable success message on successful creation (e.g., "Created note: note_5 (Meeting Notes)").
             If a duplicate title exists and forceCreate=False, returns a message describing the conflict and available options.
             On validation or input error, returns an error message starting with "Error:".
    """
    if not parentNoteId or not title or not type:
        return "Error: parentNoteId, title, and type are required."

    if type in ("file", "image") and mime is None:
        return f"Error: mime is required when type='{type}'."

    # Check duplicate title in same parent
    if not forceCreate:
        for note in notes.notes.values():
            if note.parentNoteId == parentNoteId and note.title == title:
                duplicate_id = note.noteId
                return (
                    f"Found existing note with title '{title}' in this directory.
"
                    f"Duplicate note ID: {duplicate_id}
"
                    "Please choose how to proceed:
"
                    "- Call create_note with forceCreate=True to create anyway.
"
                    f"- Or call update_note with noteId={duplicate_id} to update existing."
                )

    raw_content = content or ""
    validation = _validate_content_for_note_type(raw_content, type)
    if not validation["valid"]:
        return f"CONTENT_VALIDATION_ERROR: {validation['error']}"

    final_content = validation["content"]
    blob_id = notes.get_blob_id(final_content)
    note_id = _next_note_id(notes)
    now = _get_current_iso_time()

    new_note = Note(
        noteId=note_id,
        title=title,
        type=type,
        content=final_content,
        mime=mime,
        parentNoteId=parentNoteId,
        attributes=attributes or [],
        blobId=blob_id,
        created=now,
        updated=now
    )

    notes.notes[note_id] = new_note
    return f"Created note: {note_id} ({title})"

description: Updates an existing note's metadata and/or content in the simulated TriliumNext system. Requires the current content hash (expectedHash) to prevent concurrent modification conflicts. Supports two content update modes: 'overwrite' (replace entire content) or 'append' (add to existing content). Also validates updated content against the note's type rules.
code:
def update_note(
    notes: Annotated[NotesState, Depends("notes")],
    noteId: str,
    expectedHash: str,
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    type: Optional[NoteType] = None,
    content: Optional[str] = None,
    mime: Optional[str] = None,
    mode: Optional[Literal["overwrite", "append"]] = None,
    revision: bool = True,
) -> str:
    """
    Updates an existing note's metadata and/or content in the simulated TriliumNext system.

    Requires the current content hash (expectedHash) to prevent concurrent modification conflicts.
    Supports two content update modes: 'overwrite' (replace entire content) or 'append' (add to existing content).
    Also validates updated content against the note's type rules.

    :param noteId: The unique ID of the note to update.
    :param expectedHash: The blobId (content hash) of the note as known to the caller. Used for optimistic concurrency control.
    :param title: Optional new title for the note.
    :param type: Optional note type. Required if updating content, and must match the note's existing type or be compatible.
    :param content: Optional new content to apply based on the specified mode.
    :param mime: Optional MIME type update (only relevant for 'file'/'image' notes, though not changeable in real Trilium—supported here for completeness).
    :param mode: Required when updating content. Either 'overwrite' (replace) or 'append' (concatenate).
    :param revision: If True, simulates creation of a revision before updating (acknowledged in response but not stored).

    :return: A success message indicating what was updated (e.g., title, content mode, revision status).
             If expectedHash does not match current blobId, returns a conflict error message.
             On missing or invalid parameters, returns an "Error:" message.
    """
    if noteId not in notes.notes:
        return f"Error: Note {noteId} not found."

    if not expectedHash:
        return "Error: expectedHash is required for update."

    note = notes.notes[noteId]
    if note.blobId != expectedHash:
        return (
            f"CONFLICT: Note has been modified by another user.
"
            f"Current blobId: {note.blobId}, expected: {expectedHash}.
"
            "Please call get_note to retrieve latest content and retry."
        )

    # Metadata-only update?
    is_metadata_only = title is not None and content is None

    if is_metadata_only:
        note.title = title
        note.updated = _get_current_iso_time()
        return f"Note {noteId} title updated to '{title}'."

    # Content update requires mode and type
    if content is not None and mode not in ("overwrite", "append"):
        return "Error: mode must be 'overwrite' or 'append' when updating content."

    if content is not None and type is None:
        return "Error: type is required when updating content."

    if content is None and title is None:
        return "Error: No changes specified."

    # Validate content
    final_content = content or ""
    validation = _validate_content_for_note_type(final_content, type or note.type)
    if not validation["valid"]:
        return f"CONTENT_VALIDATION_ERROR: {validation['error']}"

    processed_content = validation["content"]

    # Apply mode
    if mode == "append":
        new_content = note.content + processed_content
    elif mode == "overwrite":
        new_content = processed_content
    else:
        new_content = note.content  # shouldn't happen

    # Update blob and content
    new_blob = notes.get_blob_id(new_content)
    note.content = new_content
    note.blobId = new_blob
    note.updated = _get_current_iso_time()

    if title is not None:
        note.title = title

    # Simulate revision (we don't store them, but acknowledge)
    revision_msg = " (revision created)" if revision else ""

    return f"Note {noteId} updated successfully.{revision_msg}"

description: Retrieves full metadata and optionally content of a note, with optional in-note text search. Returns a human-readable multi-line string containing note properties, attributes, content, and (if requested) search results with match context.
code:
def get_note(
    notes: Annotated[NotesState, Depends("notes")],
    noteId: str,
    includeContent: bool = True,
    searchPattern: Optional[str] = None,
    useRegex: bool = True,
    searchFlags: str = "g",
) -> str:
    """
    Retrieves full metadata and optionally content of a note, with optional in-note text search.

    Returns a human-readable multi-line string containing note properties, attributes, content,
    and (if requested) search results with match context.

    :param noteId: The unique ID of the note to retrieve.
    :param includeContent: If True (default), includes the note's content in the output.
    :param searchPattern: Optional pattern to search for within the note's content.
    :param useRegex: If True, treats searchPattern as a regular expression; otherwise as a literal string.
    :param searchFlags: Regex flags as a string (e.g., "gi" for global + case-insensitive). Only compatible flags are used for literal search.

    :return: A formatted string with note details, attributes, content, and search results (if applicable).
             If the note is not found, returns "Error: Note <noteId> not found."
             If the search pattern is invalid (e.g., malformed regex), includes a search error message in the output.
    """
    if noteId not in notes.notes:
        return f"Error: Note {noteId} not found."

    note = notes.notes[noteId]

    lines = [
        f"Note ID: {note.noteId}",
        f"Title: {note.title}",
        f"Type: {note.type}",
        f"Parent: {note.parentNoteId}",
        f"Created: {note.created}",
        f"Updated: {note.updated}",
        f"Blob ID: {note.blobId}",
    ]

    if note.mime:
        lines.append(f"MIME Type: {note.mime}")

    if note.attributes:
        lines.append("Attributes:")
        for attr in note.attributes:
            val = f" = {attr.value}" if attr.value else ""
            lines.append(f"  - [{attr.type}] {attr.name}{val}")

    if includeContent:
        lines.append("
Content:")
        lines.append(note.content)

        if searchPattern:
            try:
                matches = _execute_unified_search(note.content, searchPattern, useRegex, searchFlags)
                lines.append(f"
Search Results for '{searchPattern}' (regex={useRegex}, flags='{searchFlags}'):")
                if matches:
                    lines.append(f"Found {len(matches)} match(es):")
                    for i, m in enumerate(matches, 1):
                        context = note.content[max(0, m['index']-20):m['index']+m['length']+20]
                        lines.append(f"  {i}. '{m['match']}' at index {m['index']} (context: ...{context}...)")
                else:
                    lines.append("No matches found.")
            except ValueError as e:
                lines.append(f"
Search Error: {e}")

    return "
".join(lines)

description: Performs a search-and-replace operation on the content of an existing note. Requires the current content hash (expectedHash) to prevent concurrent modification conflicts. Supports both literal and regex-based replacements, with validation of the resulting content.
code:
def search_and_replace_note(
    notes: Annotated[NotesState, Depends("notes")],
    noteId: str,
    searchPattern: str,
    replacePattern: str,
    expectedHash: str,
    useRegex: bool = True,
    searchFlags: str = "g",
    revision: bool = True,
) -> str:
    """
    Performs a search-and-replace operation on the content of an existing note.

    Requires the current content hash (expectedHash) to prevent concurrent modification conflicts.
    Supports both literal and regex-based replacements, with validation of the resulting content.

    :param noteId: The unique ID of the note to modify.
    :param searchPattern: The string or regex pattern to find in the note's content.
    :param replacePattern: The string to substitute for each match (supports regex capture groups if useRegex=True).
    :param expectedHash: The expected blobId of the note's current content for concurrency safety.
    :param useRegex: If True, interprets searchPattern as a regular expression; otherwise as a literal string.
    :param searchFlags: Regex flags (e.g., "gi") to control matching behavior.
    :param revision: If True, simulates creation of a revision before applying changes (acknowledged in response).

    :return: A success message indicating number of matches/replacements and revision status.
             If no matches are found, returns a message stating no changes were made.
             If expectedHash mismatches, returns a conflict error.
             If content after replacement fails type validation, returns a CONTENT_TYPE_MISMATCH error.
             On invalid regex pattern, returns a descriptive error starting with "Search and replace error:".
    """
    if noteId not in notes.notes:
        return f"Error: Note {noteId} not found."

    if not searchPattern or not replacePattern:
        return "Error: searchPattern and replacePattern are required."

    note = notes.notes[noteId]
    if note.blobId != expectedHash:
        return (
            f"CONFLICT: Note has been modified by another user.
"
            f"Current blobId: {note.blobId}, expected: {expectedHash}.
"
            "Please call get_note to retrieve latest content and retry."
        )

    try:
        new_content, replacements = _execute_search_replace(
            note.content, searchPattern, replacePattern, useRegex, searchFlags
        )
    except ValueError as e:
        return f"Search and replace error: {e}"

    if replacements == 0:
        return f"No matches found for pattern '{searchPattern}'. No changes made."

    # Validate new content
    validation = _validate_content_for_note_type(new_content, note.type)
    if not validation["valid"]:
        return f"CONTENT_TYPE_MISMATCH: {validation['error']}"

    final_content = validation["content"]
    note.content = final_content
    note.blobId = notes.get_blob_id(final_content)
    note.updated = _get_current_iso_time()

    revision_msg = " (revision created)" if revision else ""
    return (
        f"Search and replace completed successfully.
"
        f"Found {replacements} match(es), made {replacements} replacement(s).{revision_msg}"
    )

description: Searches for notes in the simulated TriliumNext system based on text query and optional filters. Supports full-text search in note titles and content (case-insensitive substring match). Additional filters can restrict results by note type or parent folder. Mimics Trilium's search behavior in a simplified, memory-safe way. At least one search criterion must be provided.
code:
def search_notes(
    notes: Annotated[NotesState, Depends("notes")],
    query: Optional[str] = None,
    note_type: Optional[NoteType] = None,
    parent_note_id: Optional[str] = None,
    limit: Optional[int] = None,
) -> str:
    """
    Searches for notes in the simulated TriliumNext system based on text query and optional filters.

    Supports full-text search in note titles and content (case-insensitive substring match).
    Additional filters can restrict results by note type or parent folder.
    Mimics Trilium's search behavior in a simplified, memory-safe way.

    :param query: Optional text string to search for in note titles or content (case-insensitive substring match).
                  If omitted, all notes matching other filters are returned.
    :param note_type: Optional note type to filter results (e.g., 'text', 'code'). If provided, only notes of this type are included.
    :param parent_note_id: Optional parent note ID. If provided, only direct children of this parent are considered.
    :param limit: Optional maximum number of results to return. If not provided, all matching notes are returned.

    :return: A human-readable text table with columns: `noteId | title | type | parentNoteId`.
             If no notes match, returns "No notes found matching the criteria."
             If query is empty and no filters are provided, returns an error message requiring at least one search parameter.
    """
    # Enforce: at least one search criterion must be provided
    if not query and note_type is None and parent_note_id is None:
        return "Error: At least one search parameter (query, note_type, or parent_note_id) must be provided."

    results = []

    for note in notes.notes.values():
        # Apply parent filter first (cheap)
        if parent_note_id is not None and note.parentNoteId != parent_note_id:
            continue

        # Apply type filter
        if note_type is not None and note.type != note_type:
            continue

        # Apply text query (in title or content)
        if query is not None:
            q_lower = query.lower()
            if q_lower not in note.title.lower() and q_lower not in note.content.lower():
                continue

        results.append({
            "noteId": note.noteId,
            "title": note.title,
            "type": note.type,
            "parentNoteId": note.parentNoteId,
        })

    # Apply limit
    if limit is not None and limit > 0:
        results = results[:limit]

    if not results:
        return "No notes found matching the criteria."

    # Sort by noteId for deterministic output (use numeric suffix if possible)
    def extract_id_key(item):
        try:
            return int(item["noteId"].split("_")[-1])
        except (ValueError, IndexError):
            return 0

    results.sort(key=extract_id_key)

    # Build text table
    lines = ["noteId | title | type | parentNoteId"]
    for r in results:
        lines.append(f"{r['noteId']} | {r['title']} | {r['type']} | {r['parentNoteId']}")

    return "
".join(lines)
